name: trade_press_energy_flow_pressure_test_v1
objective: Test whether review-stage utility/load articles that correctly failed the capital-flow gate form a coherent energy-flow-pressure set.

items.title: PG&E data center pipeline swells to 10GW
items.reason: Large expected electricity demand increase implies future generation, transmission, substation, and transformer pressure without proving committed spend yet.

items.title: US utility Exelon reports data center pipeline of 33GW
items.reason: Very large prospective load requirement is a strong physical-demand signal even if direct capex is not yet stated.

items.title: First Energy data center pipeline surges to 2.6GW by 2029
items.reason: Data-center pipeline growth implies rising electricity-flow requirements and likely future utility investment needs.

items.title: PPL Electric's data center pipeline soars to 14GW
items.reason: Prospective large-load growth is a direct energy-input stress signal.

items.title: Exelon data center pipeline jumps to 17 GW as load forecast turns positive
items.reason: Positive load-forecast inflection indicates future pressure on the power system before explicit capital commitments are announced.

items.title: FirstEnergy’s 5-year data center pipeline doubles to 3 GW
items.reason: A doubling pipeline is strong evidence of rising energy-input demand, even if buildout is still planning-stage.

items.title: FirstEnergy expects peak load to grow 45% by 2035 on data centers
items.reason: Peak-load growth is a direct signal of energy-system stress and future infrastructure requirement.

items.title: DTE inks first data center deal to grow electric load 25%
items.reason: This one carries both energy-flow pressure and direct capital-flow relevance because the signed deal makes demand more concrete.

conclusion.reason: The review-stage utility set becomes coherent when interpreted as energy-flow pressure rather than forced direct capital-flow. This supports adding a separate upstream pressure object instead of weakening the capital-flow gate.
